Hey everyone, welcome back. So this is a video that's just going to show you the overview of what is actually inside of Jim Lawn's Trim and External Plays standpoint, meaning once you've made the decision of what you want to do, whether you have enough cash to start immediately doing external plays, meaning acquiring customers from cold traffic, or whether you're starting with the internal play first. So once you've made that decision, this is kind of the overview of how the external play piece of Jim Lawn works, and how every play that you ever received from us will include these five functions. And so this is something that you should ingrain in your mind. If you've ever got, you know, purchased something in the past and they did not have these five four functions, that's probably the reason that it was not successful for you is because usually there's a missing link. If you have all five of these things, then you will be able to be successful. So these are the five primary functions of the business. And this is kind of a thousand foot overview of how we divide up these modules. And so that you understand what you were getting into and what skills you're going to require. So first thing is lead generation, right? You need to generate inquiry. So that's going to be everything from how to how to run ads on different platforms, how to push them to a landing page, what offers to make recommendations on copy. Basically everything that has to happen prior to eyeballs becoming named for an email. Okay, so that is everything that is the lead generation component of this. Okay, and a lot of this includes the tech portion of it. So that's going to be the cool, you know, the the one that most people who are new to this will have to go through with more detail. And then after that is the second piece, which is lead nurture. So once you have the person's name for an email, you've acquired the inquiry, right from the lead generation, then you'll get into lead nurture. So that's everything that has to happen between getting the name and getting them to buy. And so typically, if we're doing an in person sale, which is what we recommend with most people starting out is you're going to have to have a reachout process in order to get them to schedule, and then a reach out process and reminder process to get them to show with the intention to purchase. Okay, so there's really kind of like two and a half pieces to it. First getting them scheduled, then getting them to show, and then the half pieces with the intention of purchasing. So it's like, how do we warm them up? How do we show them more personable? How do we get them to actually show up to their appointment set? Three is sales. Okay. And so within the process that we have, we have two two ways of selling. The first is the obvious way, which is selling the service, which is what we're, you know, which is what most people are going to do. And then we have recently updated this with a product sale. What I mean by that is that we have found actually one of our gym owners discovered this is that he was able to take all the people who said no to the service and then still bring them in, provide value and then sell them products. So he essentially was able to monetize every single person that came into the gym, whether they purchase serves from him or not. And so it literally allows you another conversion mechanism on every prospect that walks in the door, thereby allowing you to make more money and then outspend your competitors and also afford more ads than you would know what to do with. So the sales component is everything that happens from the time they show up to the time they give you money, right? And so that's going to be how to how to set up the office, how to structure the flow of the conversation, what questions to ask, and then how to ultimately close the sale. Pretty straightforward there. It's really how does the conversation go? For is fulfillment. So once you have, you know, collected money, you've closed the sale on the person, it's going to be like, how are we going to provide an amazing experience this person? And so this section is going to be everything that has to do with meal plans, grocery list, food preparation instructions, eating out guides, all of the things that we're going to provide you that you will be able to take ownership of and really over deliver. I can't stress on this enough. The people who make the most money in this program are the ones who deliver to their customers like straight up. If you think that basically if you are only making money from the front end sales from this program, then it's essentially like having a restaurant where you have people pay for the food first, and then they eat the food and then they never come back. All right, like it's not a good way to run a business. And I don't recommend it. So make sure you go through the fulfillment with your team, so that they understand how to provide exceptional service and how to ultimately retain people by providing exceptional accountability and follow through to make sure that they actually show up to their sessions. Okay. In addition here in the fulfillment area, we also do a bolt on sale for everybody who purchased service. The last piece is reselling ascension.
